description: This is a scatter plot illustrating the relationship between a central "Master" Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) image and 18 "slave" SAR images based on their acquisition time and normal baseline (B_n). The X-axis represents `Time [day]` and ranges from 0 to 630 days, with major ticks every 70 days. The Y-axis represents `B_n [m]` (normal baseline) and ranges from -150 m to +150 m, with major ticks every 50 m. The "Master" image is positioned at `Time = 350 days` and `B_n = 0 m`. Each of the 18 slave images is plotted as a blue circular point, with lines connecting each slave point directly to the Master point. The slave images are distributed temporally from approximately 50 days to 600 days and have normal baselines ranging from approximately -110 m to +140 m relative to the Master. This type of plot is fundamental for Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) processing, particularly for Persistent Scatterer Interferometry (PSI), to visualize the spatial and temporal baselines of interferometric pairs.
